summary: Round 2 fixes close Gary’s no-op bias and the live-front-door clarification, but the config validation/tests still permit non-compute routing-bias values, so the silent-no-op class from f3 remains reachable through `modes.json` edits.

findings.title: `modes.json` routing-bias values are still only checked against the full router role set, so invalid non-compute biases would pass verification and silently no-op at runtime
findings.recommendation: Validate each referenced bias profile’s `floor`/`prefer` against the compute-only set (`fast|standard|expert`), and align the fixture test to the same contract instead of the broader router role vocabulary.

findings.title: The fixture test still encodes the old broad role vocabulary, so it would approve `image`/`video`/`review` mode biases even though the runtime helper intentionally ignores them
findings.recommendation: Tighten `ROUTER_ROLES` (or rename it) to the compute-role set and keep the assertion on `MODES.routingBias` values, so CI catches the exact misconfiguration f3 was meant to eliminate.
